meta.focus: Review of Kanji, Vocabulary, and Grammar from Lessons 15–16.

sections.title: Part 1: Conversation Check
sections.instructions: Read each conversation and select the best answer.

items.title: Conversation 1
items.context: Yamamoto-sensei quizzes Rikizo about what is located in each direction from the school.

lines.jp: りきぞさん、学校の 北に 何が ありますか。
北に 駅が あります。南には 大きい 店も ありますよ。
そうですか。東と 西には 何が ありますか。
東に 高い 山が あります。西には 川が ありますよ。

items.question: Where is the station relative to the school?
items.explanation: りきぞ says 北に駅があります — 'There is a station to the north.' 北 (N5.15, きた) = north. に marks the location of existence (X に Y が あります). He also mentions 南には大きい店もあります (to the south there is also a big store), 東に高い山があります (to the east there is a tall mountain), and 西には川があります (to the west there is a river). The question asks about the station (駅, N5.5), which is to the north (北).

items.title: Conversation 2
items.context: Rikizo asks Yamamoto-sensei for help finding books in the school library.

lines.jp: 先生、新しい 本は どこに ありますか。
右に ありますよ。あの 大きい 本は 上に あります。
上ですか。左の 小さい 本は 何ですか。
それは 日本語の 本です。下にも 本が たくさん ありますよ。

items.question: Where are the new books?
items.explanation: 先生 says 右にありますよ — 'They are on the right.' 右 (N5.16, みぎ) = right. The teacher also explains that あの大きい本は上にあります (that big book is up above) — 上 (N5.16, うえ) = above/on top. りきぞ asks about 左の小さい本 (the small book on the left) — 左 (N5.16, ひだり) = left. The teacher adds 下にも本がたくさんあります (there are also many books down below) — 下 (N5.16, した) = below.

items.title: Conversation 3 (Casual)
items.context: Rikizo and Yamakawa, close friends, chat casually after school about their Japanese skills.

lines.jp: やまかわ、日本語 読むのは 上手？
うん、読むのは 上手だよ。けど、書くのは 下手だ。
わたしも 書くのが 下手だよ。読むのが いちばん すきだ。
うん。話すのも すきだけど、下手だよ。

items.question: What does Yamakawa say he is bad at?
items.explanation: やまかわ says 書くのは下手だ (I'm bad at writing) in Line 2 and 話すのも好きだけど、下手だよ (I like speaking too, but I'm bad at it) in Line 4. 下手 (N5.16, へた) = unskilled/bad at — a な-adjective. 上手 (N5.16, じょうず) = skilled/good at — its antonym. In casual speech: 上手だ/下手だ uses だ (g_da, plain copula) instead of です. けど (p_kedo, G9) = casual 'but' — やまかわ uses it to soften: 'I like it, but...' The の in 読むの/書くの/話すの is the nominalizer (p_no) turning verbs into noun phrases: 'the act of reading/writing/speaking.' Note: all lines use plain forms with no です/ます — this is consistent casual register between close friends.

sections.title: Part 2: Sentence Scramble
sections.instructions: Tap the words in the correct order to build the sentence.

items.q: There is a station to the north of the school.
items.explanation: 学校 + の (possessive: 'of the school') + 北 (N5.15, north) + に (location of existence) + 駅 + が (subject marker) + あります. に marks 北 as where the station exists (Xに Yが あります pattern). の connects 学校 to 北: 'north of the school.' 南 (N5.15) = south — wrong direction. は would mark 駅 as topic instead of が as subject; in existence statements, が is standard. です would be a copula — the sentence uses あります (existence verb for inanimate).

items.q: Please go to the right.
items.explanation: 右 (N5.16, right) + に (destination) + 行ってください (te-form of 行く + ください = polite request, G7: 'please go'). に marks 右 as the destination of movement. 行って is the te-form of 行く (godan く-verb: く → って). 左 (N5.16) = left — wrong direction. は marks a topic, not a destination. です is a copula — does not fit with the request pattern.

items.q: The big book is on top.
items.explanation: 大きい (N5.7) + 本 (N5.12) + は (topic) + 上 (N5.16, above/on top) + に (location) + あります. は marks 大きい本 as the topic ('as for the big book'). に marks 上 as where the book exists. 下 (N5.16) = below — the opposite direction. が could replace は but changes nuance to new information ('a big book is on top'). です would make this 上です = 'it is the top' — a different sentence structure.

items.q: There is a mountain to the east and a river to the west.
items.explanation: 東 (N5.15, east) + に + 山 (N5.6) + が + あって (te-form of ある, connecting two clauses, G7) + 西 (N5.15, west) + に + 川 (N5.6) + が + あります. あって is the te-form connector: 'there is X, and there is Y' — two existence statements joined by the て connector (G7, N5.5+). 北 (N5.15) = north — wrong direction. は marks topic, not subject of existence. です is a copula, not the existence verb.

items.q: I am bad at writing Japanese.
items.explanation: 日本語 + を (object) + 書くの (writing — nominalized verb: 書く + の) + が (subject marker) + 下手です (N5.16, unskilled — na-adj + です). を marks 日本語 as the object of 書く. の nominalizes the verb phrase 日本語を書く into 'the act of writing Japanese.' が marks this nominalized phrase as the subject of 下手です. 上手 (N5.16) = skilled — the opposite meaning. は could replace が but changes nuance. に marks destination/location — not needed here.

items.q: The small store is on the left.
items.explanation: 小さい (N5.7, small) + 店 (N5.5, store) + は (topic) + 左 (N5.16, left) + に (location of existence) + あります. は marks 小さい店 as the topic. に marks 左 as where the store exists. 右 (N5.16) = right — wrong direction. が could replace は (changes nuance to new information). です would make 左です = 'it is the left' — a copula sentence, not an existence statement.

items.q: She is very good at speaking Japanese.
items.explanation: 日本語 + を (object) + 話すの (speaking — nominalized verb: 話す + の) + が (subject) + とても (very) + 上手です (N5.16, skilled — na-adj + です). を marks 日本語 as what is spoken. の nominalizes 日本語を話す into a noun phrase. が marks this as the subject of 上手です. とても (N5.7) = very — adverb intensifying 上手. 下手 (N5.16) = unskilled — opposite meaning. は could replace が. に is a location/destination particle — does not fit.

sections.title: Part 3: Reading — Directions Around Town
sections.instructions: Read the passage and answer the questions.

passage.jp: わたしは 毎日 学校に 行きます。
passage.en: I go to school every day.

passage.jp: 学校の 北に 駅が あります。毎日 駅から 学校に 行きます。
passage.en: There is a station to the north of the school. I go from the station to school every day.

passage.jp: 東に 高い 山が あります。西に 川が あります。
passage.en: There is a tall mountain to the east. There is a river to the west.

passage.jp: 南に 大きい 店が あります。新しい 本を 買いたいですから、よく 南に 行きます。
passage.en: There is a big store to the south. Because I want to buy new books, I often go south.

passage.jp: 店の 中で 右に 本が あります。左には 花が あります。
passage.en: Inside the store, books are on the right. Flowers are on the left.

questions.q: 学校の 東に 何が ありますか。
questions.a: 高い 山が あります。
choices: 高い 山が あります。
大きい 店が あります。
questions.explanation: Line 3 states 東に高い山があります — 'There is a tall mountain to the east.' 東 (N5.15, ひがし) = east. 高い (N5.8) = tall/high. 川 (river) is to the west (西). 大きい店 (big store) is to the south (南). 駅 (station) is to the north (北). The に marks location of existence.

questions.q: どうして よく 南に 行きますか。
questions.a: 新しい 本を 買いたいからです。
choices: 新しい 本を 買いたいからです。
駅が 南に あるからです。
山を 見たいからです。
学校が 南に あるからです。
questions.explanation: Line 4 states 新しい本を買いたいですから、よく南に行きます — 'Because I want to buy new books, I often go south.' 買いたいです (desire_tai, G8) = 'want to buy' — 買う (N5.8) in desire form. から (p_kara_because, G9) = 'because' after a verb/adjective. よく (N5.7) = often. 南 (N5.15, みなみ) = south. The other choices incorrectly place the station, mountain, or school to the south.

questions.q: 店の 中で 本は どこに ありますか。
questions.explanation: Line 5 states 店の中で右に本があります — 'Inside the store, books are on the right.' 右 (N5.16, みぎ) = right. 中 (N5.9) = inside. で marks the location of the action/situation. 左 (N5.16, ひだり) = left — that is where the flowers (花, N5.11) are. 上 (N5.16, うえ) and 下 (N5.16, した) are not mentioned for the store interior.

sections.title: Part 4: Kanji Readings
sections.instructions: Select the correct reading for the [bracketed] word.

items.q: 学校の [北] に 駅が あります。
items.explanation: 北 (north) is read as kita (kun-reading). 北 (ほく/きた) — kun-reading きた. On-reading ほく is used in compounds like 北西 (ほくせい = north-west) and 北東 (ほくとう = north-east). Compare: 南 (みなみ = south), 東 (ひがし = east), 西 (にし = west) — all four cardinal-direction kanji from N5.15.

items.q: [南] に 大きい 店が あります。
items.explanation: 南 (south) is read as minami (kun-reading). 南 (なん/みなみ) — kun-reading みなみ. On-reading なん is used in compounds like 南西 (なんせい = south-west) and 南東 (なんとう = south-east). Compare: 北 (きた = north), 西 (にし = west), 東 (ひがし = east) — all N5.15 cardinal directions.

items.q: [東] に 山が あります。
items.explanation: 東 (east) is read as higashi (kun-reading). 東 (とう/ひがし) — kun-reading ひがし. On-reading とう is used in compounds like 北東 (ほくとう = north-east) and 南東 (なんとう = south-east). The kanji is also found in 東京 (とうきょう = Tokyo, N4.17). Compare: 西 (にし = west), 南 (みなみ = south), 北 (きた = north).

items.q: [西] に 川が あります。
items.explanation: 西 (west) is read as nishi (kun-reading). 西 (せい/にし) — kun-reading にし. On-reading せい is used in compounds like 北西 (ほくせい = north-west) and 南西 (なんせい = south-west). Compare: 東 (ひがし = east), 北 (きた = north), 南 (みなみ = south) — all N5.15 cardinal directions.

items.q: [右] に 本が あります。
items.explanation: 右 (right) is read as migi (kun-reading). 右 (ゆう/みぎ) — kun-reading みぎ. On-reading ゆう is used in formal compounds like 右折 (うせつ = right turn). Compare: 左 (ひだり = left), 上 (うえ = above), 下 (した = below) — all N5.16 positional kanji.

items.q: [左] の 小さい 本は 何ですか。
items.explanation: 左 (left) is read as hidari (kun-reading). 左 (さ/ひだり) — kun-reading ひだり. On-reading さ is used in compounds like 左右 (さゆう = left and right). Compare: 右 (みぎ = right), 下 (した = below), 上 (うえ = above) — all N5.16 positional kanji.

items.q: あの 本は [上] に あります。
items.explanation: 上 (above / on top) is read as ue (kun-reading). 上 (じょう/うえ) — kun-reading うえ. On-reading じょう is used in compounds like 上手 (じょうず = skilled, N5.16). Compare: 下 (した = below), 右 (みぎ = right), 左 (ひだり = left) — all N5.16 positional kanji.

items.q: [下] にも 本が たくさん あります。
items.explanation: 下 (below / underneath) is read as shita (kun-reading). 下 (か/げ/した) — kun-reading した. On-reading か/げ is used in compounds like 下手 (へた = unskilled, N5.16) and 地下 (ちか = underground, N4.19). Compare: 上 (うえ = above), 左 (ひだり = left), 右 (みぎ = right) — all N5.16 positional kanji.

sections.title: Part 5: Context & Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Select the best word to complete the sentence.

items.q: 日本語を 書くのが _______ です。（I am good at writing Japanese.）
items.explanation: 上手 (N5.16, じょうず) = skilled/good at — a な-adjective describing ability. 上手です = 'is good at.' 下手 (N5.16, へた) = unskilled/bad at — the antonym; would change the meaning to 'bad at writing Japanese.' 上 (N5.16, うえ) = above — a positional noun, not a skill description. 下 (N5.16, した) = below — a positional noun. Note: 上手 uses the on-reading of 上 (じょう), not the kun-reading (うえ). Similarly, 下手 uses the on-reading of 下 (へ/げ, as in へた), not した.

items.q: 学校の _______ に 山が あります。（There is a mountain to the east of the school.）
items.explanation: 東 (N5.15, ひがし) = east — matches the English 'to the east.' The pattern 学校の東に = 'to the east of the school.' 西 (にし) = west, 北 (きた) = north, 南 (みなみ) = south — all cardinal directions (N5.15) but wrong for this sentence. の connects 学校 (school) to the direction word; に marks the location of existence.

items.q: _______ に 行ってください。（Please go to the left.）
items.explanation: 左 (N5.16, ひだり) = left — matches the English 'to the left.' 行ってください = te-form of 行く + ください = polite request 'please go' (G7). に marks 左 as the destination. 右 (みぎ) = right — wrong direction. 上 (うえ) = above — 上に行ってください = 'please go up.' 下 (した) = below — 下に行ってください = 'please go down.' All are valid directions (N5.16), but the English specifies left.

items.q: 北東は 北と _______ です。（North-east is north and _____.）
items.explanation: 東 (N5.15, ひがし) = east — 北東 (ほくとう, N5.15) is a compound direction meaning 'north-east,' combining 北 (ほく, north) + 東 (とう, east). The compound uses on-readings: ほく + とう = ほくとう. と connects 北 and 東 as 'and.' Similarly: 南西 (なんせい) = south + west, 北西 (ほくせい) = north + west, 南東 (なんとう) = south + east. 西 (にし) would make 北西 (north-west). 南 (みなみ) would make 南東 (not 北東). 右 (みぎ) is a positional direction, not a cardinal direction used in compounds.

sections.title: Part 6: Grammar Check
sections.instructions: Choose the correct particle or form to complete the sentence.

items.q: 東 ___ 山が あります。（There is a mountain to the east.）
items.explanation: に (p_ni) marks the location of existence — 東に山があります = 'there is a mountain to the east.' The pattern Xに Yが あります uses に for static existence of inanimate objects. で marks a location where an ACTION takes place (東で食べます = 'I eat in the east'). を marks a direct object — not a location. は marks the topic — 東は山があります changes nuance to 'as for the east, there is a mountain.'

items.q: 右 ___ 行ってください。（Please go to the right.）
items.explanation: に (p_ni) marks the destination/direction of movement — 右に行ってください = 'Please go to the right.' 行ってください = te-form of 行く + ください = polite request (G7). で marks a location of an action ('at/in the right') — grammatically awkward here because 行く describes movement to a destination. が marks a subject — 右が行って makes no sense. の is possessive — 右の would need a noun after it (右の本 = 'the book on the right').

items.q: 新しい 本を 買いたいです ___、南に 行きます。（Because I want to buy new books, I go south.）
items.explanation: から (p_kara_because, G9) after a verb/adjective/です gives a reason: 'because.' 買いたいですから = 'because I want to buy.' 買いたいです (desire_tai, G8) = 'want to buy.' が (p_ga_but) would mean 'but' — 買いたいですが = 'I want to buy, but...' — contradicts the English meaning 'because.' けど (p_kedo, G9) is casual 'but' — same issue. も (p_mo) = 'also/too' — does not create a causal link.

items.q: 学校は 小さいです ___、北に 駅が あります。（The school is small, but there is a station to the north.）
items.explanation: が (p_ga_but, G9) is the polite clause-connector 'but' — it follows a polite form (小さいです) to contrast: 'the school is small, BUT there is a station to the north.' This が is p_ga_but (conjunction 'but') — position determines role: が after a clause-final form (です/ます) = 'but.' Note: the second が in 駅が is p_ga (subject marker after a noun). から (p_kara_because) would mean 'because the school is small' — wrong meaning. に marks location — already used in 北に. も = 'also' — does not create a contrast.
